A11y Cookbook

  • Overview
    • How to test ?
        • Button
        • Link
        • Heading
        • Image
        • Table
        • List
        • Playground
        • Label
        • Input
        • Textarea
        • Content Editable
        • Checkbox
        • Radio Group
        • Switch
        • aria-label
        • aria-labelledby
        • aria-describedby
        • aria-expanded
        • aria-current
        • aria-live
        • Modal
        • Accordion
        • Tabs

      Switch

      A switch allows users to turn a setting on or off.

      A switch is similar to a checkbox, but communicates a binary state such as:

      • Enabled / Disabled
      • On / Off
      • Active / Inactive

      Use a switch when the action immediately affects a setting.

      Example

      export function Switch() {
  return (
    <label className="flex items-center gap-2">
      <input type="checkbox" role="switch" />
      Email Notifications
    </label>
  );
}

      Accessibility Notes

      • Use a visible label.
      • Use role="switch" when representing an on/off setting.
      • Keep the checked state synchronized with the UI.
      • Prefer native form controls over custom implementations.

      Basic Switch

      <label>
  <input type="checkbox" role="switch" />

  Email Notifications
</label>

      Screen reader:

      Email Notifications
switch
off

      When checked:

      Email Notifications
switch
on

      Required Switch

      <label>
  <input type="checkbox" role="switch" required />

  Enable Security Features
</label>

      Screen reader:

      Enable Security Features
required
switch
off

      Switch with Description

      <label>
  <input type="checkbox" role="switch" aria-describedby="notification-help" />

  Email Notifications
</label>

<p id="notification-help">Receive updates about your account activity.</p>

      Screen reader:

      Email Notifications
switch
off

Receive updates about your account activity.

      Switch with Error

      <label>
  <input
    type="checkbox"
    role="switch"
    aria-invalid="true"
    aria-describedby="notification-error"
  />

  Email Notifications
</label>

<p id="notification-error">You must enable notifications before continuing.</p>

      Screen reader:

      Email Notifications
invalid entry
switch

You must enable notifications before continuing.

      Checked State

      <input type="checkbox" role="switch" checked />

      Screen reader:

      switch
on

      Unchecked State

      <input type="checkbox" role="switch" />

      Screen reader:

      switch
off

      Switch vs Checkbox

      These controls are visually similar but communicate different intent.

      Checkbox

      <input type="checkbox" />

      Screen reader:

      checkbox
checked

      Use for:

      • Selecting options
      • Multi-select forms
      • Preferences

      Examples:

      ☑ Email
☑ SMS
☐ Push Notifications

      Switch

      <input type="checkbox" role="switch" />

      Screen reader:

      switch
on

      Use for:

      • Feature toggles
      • Settings
      • Enable / Disable actions

      Examples:

      Dark Mode
On

Email Notifications
Off

      Keyboard Support

      Native switches inherit keyboard support from checkboxes.

      KeyAction
      TabFocus switch
      Shift + TabMove to previous element
      SpaceToggle switch

      Common Mistakes

      ❌ Clickable Div

      <div class="switch" onclick="toggle()"></div>

      Problems:

      • Not keyboard accessible.
      • No semantic meaning.
      • Screen readers do not recognize it as a switch.

      ✅ Native Control

      <input type="checkbox" role="switch" />

      Provides:

      • Keyboard support
      • Screen reader support
      • Form integration

      ❌ Missing Label

      <input type="checkbox" role="switch" />

      Screen readers may announce:

      switch
off

      without explaining what the switch controls.

      ✅ Visible Label

      <label>
  <input type="checkbox" role="switch" />

  Email Notifications
</label>

      Screen reader:

      Email Notifications
switch
off

      ❌ State Not Updated

      <input type="checkbox" role="switch" aria-checked="true" />

      while the visual UI shows OFF.

      Assistive technologies and visual users receive conflicting information.

      ✅ Keep State Synchronized

      <input type="checkbox" role="switch" checked />

      The visual and accessible states should always match.

      When to Use

      Use a switch when users are toggling a setting between two states.

      Examples:

      • Dark Mode
      • Email Notifications
      • Auto Save
      • Two-Factor Authentication
      • Location Services

      Prefer a checkbox when users are selecting options in a form.

      Think of it as:

      Checkbox = Select this option

Switch = Turn this setting on or off
